subject: Social Science (History)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the major challenges faced by India after independence in 1947
Recognize the importance of the Constitution in building a democratic nation
Understand the reorganization of states in India
Identify efforts made for economic and social development
Recognize the role of leaders in shaping modern India
Understand the importance of unity in a diverse country
Explain the progress and challenges of independent India

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Independent India
student_activities: List challenges faced by India after independence

lesson_plan.title: Making of the Indian Constitution
topics_covered: Role of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar
student_activities: Read short sections from the textbook
Discuss the importance of the Constitution

lesson_plan.title: Reorganization of States
student_activities: Discuss why language was used as a basis for states

lesson_plan.title: Economic Development After Independence
student_activities: Discuss why development planning was needed

lesson_plan.title: Social Changes in Independent India
student_activities: Write a short paragraph on the importance of education

lesson_plan.title: Unity in Diversity
student_activities: List examples of diversity in India
Discuss why unity is important for the country

teacher_tip: Use maps, timelines, and real-life examples to explain the development of India after independence. Encourage students to connect historical developments with present-day India to make learning more meaningful.
